title: Swapping two inputs flips the sign of the Nyquist mode

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The Nyquist mode of a two-point signal changes sign when the two entries are swapped.
assumptions: u0 and u1 are real numbers
the Nyquist mode is the difference u0 - u1
informal.expected_conclusion: Swapping entries negates the Nyquist mode.

declarations.name: dft2Nyquist
declarations.statement: def dft2Nyquist (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := u0 - u1

declarations.name: dft2Nyquist_swap
declarations.statement: theorem dft2Nyquist_swap (u0 u1 : Real) : dft2Nyquist u1 u0 = -dft2Nyquist u0 u1

tasks.input: Translate sign-flip symmetry of the two-point Nyquist mode into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem dft2Nyquist_swap (u0 u1 : Real) : dft2Nyquist u1 u0 = -dft2Nyquist u0 u1

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding dft2Nyquist and simplifying.
